findings.evidence: BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY ----***-- in /projects/treatment_logic_v4.acd.bak (Rockwell Studio 5000 project backup)
aaUser:Wonder***24 in /etc/aveva/historian.cfg (AVEVA Historian admin account, plaintext)
ftview_admin:plant***42 in /exports/panelview-treatment.xml — note: may be vendor default in export template; verify against deployed credential before action
Integrator CI workflow ci.yml uses unpinned actions/checkout@master — finding owner is the system integrator that builds your Studio 5000 projects, not the utility ops team
AVEVA System Platform 2023 R2 dependency log4j-core 2.14.1 (CVE-2021-44228) located in /opt/aveva/lib — vendor patch path required, network isolation as compensating control
Outdated OpenSSL 1.0.2k bundled with DNP3 connector library at /opt/aveva/dnp3 — exposure depends on whether DNP3 traffic terminates outside the OT DMZ
TCP/44818 (EtherNet/IP CIP) on SCADA Primary reachable from corporate VLAN per simulated Shodan banner — violates IT/OT segmentation
TCP/20000 (DNP3) on Lift Station 7 RTU responding on cellular carrier IP (Verizon Private LTE class) without source-IP allowlist — common mid-size-utility lift-station exposure pattern
HTTP/80 FactoryTalk View web UI exposed with default Rockwell self-signed cert; restrict to management VLAN, replace cert

GuardLogix chemical-dosing safety controller a_sis_01 reachable from z_treatment (operator HMI zone) — violates IEC 62443 zone isolation; risk to chlorine residual safety interlock and finished-water quality
